emails.subject: Hargrove billing discrepancy - need a second set of eyes
emails.content:
Marcus,

Following up on what I flagged to Denise earlier this month. I've gone through the full QuickBooks history for Hargrove Capital Partners (HRGV-4401).

Bigger than I thought. Their advisory agreement from March 2024 puts Account HRGV-4401-B on the fee-exempt list. But starting Q2 2025, that account was billed at the standard advisory rate. Three quarters of charges that shouldn't be there:

- Q2 2025: $4,500
- Q3 2025: $4,750
- Q4 2025: $4,750

Total overcharge: $14,000

I've checked the client agreement twice. The fee-exempt status is clear. Someone switched the account to standard tier in the billing system around April/May 2025 and it was never caught.

Can you pull the change log for that account? I want the full picture before I bring this to Denise formally.

Christine

emails.subject: Re: Hargrove billing discrepancy - need a second set of eyes
emails.content:
Christine,

Just pulled the change log. Account was reclassified April 14, 2025. System shows it as a bulk update, looks like it got caught in the Q2 rate schedule refresh. Probably wasn't intentional, just a batch job that didn't exclude the exempt accounts.

Your numbers match what I'm seeing. $14K is over the reporting threshold so this needs to go to Denise. Want me to sit in when you present it?

Marcus

emails.subject: Q1 Audit Finding - Hargrove Capital Partners billing discrepancy ($14,000)
emails.content:
Denise,

Attached is the formal finding from the Q1 audit cycle.

Hargrove Capital Partners, Account HRGV-4401-B, was incorrectly charged advisory fees totaling $14,000 across Q2-Q4 2025. The account is designated fee-exempt per the client agreement dated March 15, 2024. The billing error originated from a bulk rate schedule update on April 14, 2025 that did not exclude exempt accounts.

Breakdown:
- Q2 2025: $4,500
- Q3 2025: $4,750
- Q4 2025: $4,750

The error is systemic (data entry, not intentional). Marcus confirmed via the change log. No other exempt accounts were affected across my three assigned portfolios.

Recommendation: client notification, full refund, and a review of the batch update process to prevent recurrence.

Happy to walk through whenever works for you.

Christine

emails.subject: Re: Q1 Audit Finding - Hargrove Capital Partners billing discrepancy ($14,000)
emails.content:
Good catch. This needs to go to the client. Let's meet Thursday at 3 to draft the notification letter and discuss the refund process. Bring Marcus.

Denise

emails.subject: Q2 coverage planning — schedule input needed
emails.content:
Team,

As we wrap up Q1, I want to get ahead of Q2 coverage planning. The next audit cycle starts April 13 and I need to make sure we have adequate coverage through June.

Please send me your Q2 schedules by Monday, including:
- Planned PTO dates
- Recurring appointments during business hours (doctor's visits, childcare pickups, etc.)
- Any standing personal commitments that might affect your availability

I know this is more detail than I usually ask for, but after the coverage gaps we had in February I want to plan proactively. The earlier I know, the easier it is to schedule around.

Thanks,
Denise
